description.value:
Elysmera is said to have been born at the first dawn of the world, rising from the Life Stream as the sun kissed Elara’s skies for the first time. She was not formed from flame, but chose it—not to burn, but to warm, to guide, to cleanse. Her earliest act was to lift the cold from the land and ignite courage in the hearts of mortals.

It is said her tears formed the first phoenix, a symbol of rebirth and undying hope.

She is the eternal adversary of the corrupted, especially undead and aberrations, whom she sees as anathema to life and truth. Her flame is gentle for the repentant—but for the defiant and malicious, it burns with cleansing fury.
